Rule Description: Map all source status variants to Silver standard: APPROVED or PENDING. SAP: APPROVED/PENDING (direct). Oracle: VALIDATED→APPROVED. Baan: POSTED→APPROVED. Workday: Approved→APPROVED, In Review→PENDING.
Decision / Owner: Confirmed by Sarah Chen 2025-05-20. Implement as CASE statement in Silver DT.

Rule Description: Do NOT convert currencies at the Silver layer. Silver stores the original transaction currency. Currency conversion happens in Gold using the corporate FX rate table maintained by Treasury.
Decision / Owner: Confirmed by Marcus Reinhardt and Jennifer Okafor. Treasury FX table is out of scope for this project.

Rule Description: Baan has a known issue: nightly extract sometimes sends the same invoice twice with different BAN_INVOICE_ID values but identical BAN_INVOICE_REF. Deduplicate on INVOICE_NUMBER using latest BAN_CREATED timestamp. Use QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY INVOICE_NUMBER ORDER BY CREATED_AT DESC) = 1.
Decision / Owner: Reported by Karen van der Berg 2025-05-15. Engineering to implement. Silver-layer concern not Bronze.

Rule Description: Flag invoices with INVOICE_AMOUNT > $500,000 USD equivalent for manual review. NOT a filter — do not drop rows. Add a data quality check that alerts when count of high-value invoices exceeds expectations.
Decision / Owner: Requested by Finance Controller Tom Walsh 2025-06-01. Implement as a DMF in the Guardrails Pack not in the Silver DT itself.

Rule Description: Inconsistent formatting: SAP=NET30/NET60, Oracle=N30/N60, Baan=N30/N60, Workday=Net 30/Net 60. OPEN QUESTION: normalize at Silver or Gold layer?
Decision / Owner: NEEDS DECISION — Sarah to discuss with David Kim by 2025-06-20. Current recommendation: leave as-is at Silver normalize at Gold.

Rule Description: Each source uses a different GL account numbering scheme. Do NOT attempt to create a unified chart of accounts at Silver. Silver stores the original GL code as-is. Gold layer will join to the CoA mapping table (future phase).
Decision / Owner: Confirmed by Tom Walsh and Sarah Chen. CoA mapping is Phase 2 (Q4 2025).

Rule Description: Every row in Silver must include a SOURCE_SYSTEM column: 'SAP', 'ORACLE', 'BAAN', or 'WORKDAY'. Hard-coded literal in each UNION ALL branch.
Decision / Owner: Established pattern from Phase 1. Continue for new sources.

Rule Description: System-specific columns dropped at bronze-to-silver boundary: SAP (SAP_COMPANY_CODE, SAP_DOCUMENT_TYPE), Oracle (ORACLE_ORG_ID, ORACLE_SOURCE), Baan (BAN_COMPANY), Workday (WD_TENANT_ID). Join back to Bronze using INVOICE_ID + SOURCE_SYSTEM if needed.

Source data arrives at different times: SAP=real-time CDC, Oracle=hourly batch, Baan=nightly ~02:00 UTC, Workday=hourly. Silver DT uses TARGET_LAG = DOWNSTREAM. Gold DT proposed at 2 hours — revisit after 30 days of monitoring.
Decision / Owner: Engineering decision. No action needed — DT handles this.

Rule Description: Bronze tables retain all historical data. Silver and Gold are current-state views (Dynamic Tables). For historical analysis query Bronze directly or create a separate time-series table.
Decision / Owner: Noted by Sarah Chen 2025-06-12. Add to Phase 2 backlog.
